description_long: This object consists of three cylindrical metal barrels, each featuring a prominent yellow and black radioactive warning symbol on their sides. The barrels are colored in dark teal, deep red, and black, with each color corresponding to a different barrel. The surfaces appear slightly worn and matte, with visible horizontal banding that reinforces the structure. The tops of the barrels are flat with a small circular cap or bung, suggesting they are sealed. These barrels are typically used for storing hazardous or radioactive materials and are commonly found in industrial or restricted urban environments. The overall construction suggests durability and containment, with no visible handles or openings aside from the top cap.
description: Three reinforced metal barrels in teal, red, and black, each marked with a radioactive warning symbol and sealed with a top cap.
description_view_0: Front view showing the barrels aligned in a row, with the teal barrel in front, followed by red and black; radioactive symbols are partially visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying all three barrels side by side, with their radioactive warning symbols clearly visible on each barrel.
description_view_2: Rear view with the black barrel in front, followed by red and teal; the tops of the barrels are visible, showing the sealed caps.
description_view_3: Opposite side view to view 1, again showing all three barrels side by side with radioactive symbols facing forward.
